topics.name: AI Foundry
topics.description: Microsoft Foundry — model catalog, Agent Service, Agent Framework, Foundry Toolkit, hosted agents, evaluations, fine-tuning

topics.name: AI Infrastructure
topics.description: Azure AI infrastructure — Maia/Cobalt silicon, Manifold, Context Cache, prompt caching, inference optimization, containers

topics.name: Models
topics.description: Frontier and open models — MAI family, GPT-5.x, Claude/Anthropic, Llama, Mistral, Phi, Aurora geospatial, Image 2.5

topics.name: Agents
topics.description: Agent platforms and patterns — Agent 365, Autopilot, Scout, Claw + Agent Harness, OpenClaw, MCP, Toolbox, ACS, ASSERT

topics.name: Windows
topics.description: Windows for developers and agents — WSL Containers, Surface RTX Spark, Copilot+ PCs, on-device AI, Windows ML

topics.name: Developer Tools
topics.description: GitHub + VS Code + GitHub Copilot — CLI, Copilot app, Auto Fix, Azure DevOps integration, agentic SDLC

topics.name: Security + Governance
topics.description: Agent governance and security — Agent 365, Purview, Defender, Entra, ASSERT, ACS, evaluation frameworks

items.title: Japan Wrap-up Session
items.sentence: The host explains that this 45-minute session will summarize the most important announcements from Build 2026 and present them clearly in Japanese for both livestream and local attendees (00:00:25–00:01:00).

items.title: Japan Wrap-up Session
items.sentence: Experts from different fields at Microsoft Japan are introduced, and the first speaker, Executive Officer Okazaki, takes the stage to discuss Build 2026’s primary themes and announcements.

items.title: Japan Wrap-up Session
items.sentence: Microsoft introduced new chipsets—Maia 200 and Cobalt 200—designed to enhance energy efficiency and AI workload processing across cloud and edge (00:05:29–00:06:04).

items.title: Japan Wrap-up Session
items.sentence: Database breakthroughs, like Azure’s HorizonDB public preview and GPU-accelerated Fabric Data Warehouse, were showcased to strengthen enterprise-scale data operations (00:06:45–00:07:23).

items.title: Japan Wrap-up Session
items.sentence: Yamamoto later discusses infrastructure scaling, highlighting Microsoft’s AI data center expansion focused on sustainability (Fairwater project), modernization support through Azure migration Copilot for mainframe-inclusive architectures, and the unveiling of Layfin—a framework that allows rapid AI-powered backend construction for production-ready applications (00:41:04–00:45:03).

items.title: Idea to production-ready agent in seconds on AI-native runtime
items.speakers: Simon Jakesch, Devanshi Joshi, Gopi Prashanth
items.sentence: The demo validates cold start under 100 milliseconds, sandbox persistence, connector inheritance, and strong network isolation—key architectural features of Azure Container Apps sandboxes in public preview.

items.title: Idea to production-ready agent in seconds on AI-native runtime
items.speakers: Simon Jakesch, Devanshi Joshi, Gopi Prashanth
items.sentence: Product Announcements and Customer Use Cases: Transitioning back, Devanshi announces Azure Container Apps Sandboxes and Container Apps Express as a fast, isolated compute infrastructure executing workloads securely by default (00:27:00–00:29:00).

items.title: Idea to production-ready agent in seconds on AI-native runtime
items.speakers: Simon Jakesch, Devanshi Joshi, Gopi Prashanth
items.sentence: Closing Insights and Q&A: Devanshi concludes the session summarizing that container apps and sandboxes are transforming AI agent production pipelines, now available in public preview (00:41:12).

items.title: The honest practitioner's take on agentic AI on Kubernetes
items.sentence: He announces the public preview of "Anyscale on Azure" (00:25:15), a managed Ray service jointly developed with Anyscale, integrated with Azure’s identity and billing ecosystem.

items.title: The honest practitioner's take on agentic AI on Kubernetes
items.sentence: Announcements, Real-World Cases, and Closing Takeaways: Near 00:42:12, Lachlan announces new AKS features: Automatic node pools for simplified scaling, Azure Container Linux for secure, minimal OS footprints, AKS on bare metal for virtualization-free performance, and Fleet for managing multi-cluster topologies.

items.title: PepsiCo’s blueprint for agentic AI
items.speakers: Krunal Patel, Rishabh Saha, Bob Ward, Govanna Flores
items.sentence: They introduce the central challenge faced by key account managers (Cams): fragmented information across numerous systems and reports, described vividly between 00:09:04–00:10:00.

items.title: Build smarter AI systems in Foundry as models and costs evolve
items.speakers: Yina Arenas, Naomi Moneypenny, Sharmila Chockalingam
items.sentence: Yina introduces custom and rubric-based evaluation tools now available in Foundry (00:25:00), complementing built-in quality, safety, and compliance evaluators.

items.title: Build smarter AI systems in Foundry as models and costs evolve
items.speakers: Yina Arenas, Naomi Moneypenny, Sharmila Chockalingam
items.sentence: She announces explicit prompt caching — a new Azure capability granting developers private, persistent caching layers to cut latency and token costs (00:32:00).

items.title: Deploy. Observe. Learn. Reinforcement learning for production agents
items.speakers: Alicia Frame, Omkar More, Long Chen
items.sentence: They outline the talk’s flow—covering fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, demos, and the upcoming low-level training API.

items.title: Deploy. Observe. Learn. Reinforcement learning for production agents
items.speakers: Alicia Frame, Omkar More, Long Chen
items.sentence: Demo 3 – Low-Level API and Interactive Training: The third demo 00:33:10 introduces Foundry’s upcoming interactive training API—a low-level interface granting data scientists direct control over algorithms without managing GPU infrastructure.

items.title: Post-Training and Deploying Open Source Reasoning Models in Foundry
items.speakers: Vijay Aski, Manoj Bableshwar, Chris Lauren
items.sentence: Evaluation dashboards reveal how smaller, post-trained models catch up with large proprietary ones in quality while reducing cost.

items.title: Post-Training and Deploying Open Source Reasoning Models in Foundry
items.speakers: Vijay Aski, Manoj Bableshwar, Chris Lauren
items.sentence: A new API, tentatively called “Loom,” simplifies reinforcement and supervised tuning on CPUs without configuring GPU clusters.

items.title: Software Defensibility in the era of AI coding
items.sentence: The talk closes with excitement about upcoming robotics demonstrations and Chip’s vision of AI-enhanced physical systems still grounded by human purpose and defensible expertise 00:42:36.

items.title: From prototype to production: build and run agents at scale
items.speakers: Tina Schuchman, Jeff Hollan, Takuto Higuchi
items.sentence: Introduction and Vision for AI Agents: The video begins with Tina Schackman and Jeff Holland introducing themselves and outlining the session’s purpose—showing how to build, deploy, and operate enterprise-level AI agents using Microsoft Foundry (00:00:00–00:00:27).

items.title: From prototype to production: build and run agents at scale
items.speakers: Tina Schuchman, Jeff Hollan, Takuto Higuchi
items.sentence: The Foundry Toolkit now generally available helps developers integrate tracing, evaluations, and best practices directly in the coding environment (00:09:54–00:10:04).

items.title: From prototype to production: build and run agents at scale
items.speakers: Tina Schuchman, Jeff Hollan, Takuto Higuchi
items.sentence: Deploying and Hosting Enterprise Agents at Scale: Tina follows up announcing production-ready releases: Microsoft Agent Framework v1.0, Foundry Toolkit in VS Code, and upcoming Foundry hosted agents with secure sandbox isolation and long-running support (00:18:02–00:19:59).

items.title: Turn your agents into action: Connect tools, APIs, and documents
items.speakers: Ronak Chokshi, Joe Filcik, Maria Naggaga
items.sentence: In Foundry, she uploads a reusable skill and introduces browser automation — a new capability for scraping and form filling built on Playwright (00:17:00–00:18:06).

items.title: Turn your agents into action: Connect tools, APIs, and documents
items.speakers: Ronak Chokshi, Joe Filcik, Maria Naggaga
items.sentence: Content Understanding and Real-World Applications: Joe Flick takes over, introducing “Content Understanding” as the solution for agents handling complex multimodal content outside APIs such as documents, images, and videos (00:22:31–00:23:07).

items.title: Turn your agents into action: Connect tools, APIs, and documents
items.speakers: Ronak Chokshi, Joe Filcik, Maria Naggaga
items.sentence: He introduces “Agentic Mode,” an upcoming July enhancement for reasoning across documents to derive complex answers rather than just extracting values (00:31:01–00:33:18).

items.title: Claw and agent harness in Microsoft Foundry
items.speakers: Glenn Condron, Amanda Foster, Shawn Henry
items.sentence: Amanda then introduces a new generation called “Autopilot agents” (00:39:46), which act autonomously with their own user identities, allowing them to send messages, manage documents, and perform independent actions traditionally limited to human accounts.

items.title: Foundry IQ: Fuel agents with enterprise knowledge and agentic retrieval
items.sentence: He transitions to the provisioning process and announces an important milestone—the public preview of the **serverless version of Foundry IQ** (00:09:03).

items.title: Foundry IQ: Fuel agents with enterprise knowledge and agentic retrieval
items.sentence: This new capability allows developers to create services in 10–20 seconds, with auto-scaling and pay-per-use flexibility.

items.title: Observe and control agents across any framework with open source tools
items.speakers: Sarah Bird, Sandeep Atluri, Mehrnoosh Sameki, Katelyn Rothney
items.sentence: Future Frontiers and Responsible Collaboration: In closing, Byrd and Sandeep preview upcoming advancements including continuous evaluations powered by reinforcement-learning attackers that adapt tests based on production performance 00:37:03–00:39:20.

items.title: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
items.speakers: Vivek Bhadauria, Sebastian Kohlmeier, Filisha Shah
items.sentence: Multi-Turn Evaluation and Continuous Improvement: The session continues with announcements of advanced public preview features—multi-turn evaluation and user simulation 00:17:48.

items.title: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
items.speakers: Vivek Bhadauria, Sebastian Kohlmeier, Filisha Shah
items.sentence: Demo 2: Agent Optimization at Scale: Vivek takes the stage to showcase “Agent Optimizer,” a new feature designed for iterative, automated optimization of AI agents based on rubrics and datasets 00:21:01.

items.title: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
items.speakers: Vivek Bhadauria, Sebastian Kohlmeier, Filisha Shah
items.sentence: The presentation closes with upcoming session invitations and encouragement to explore the Foundry hands-on labs available at Build 00:35:19.

items.title: Build Apps w\/ Local AI for Unmetered Intelligence on every Windows PC
items.speakers: Jordi Janer, Aditi Narvekar, Anastasiya Tarnouskaya, Tucker Burns
items.sentence: The narrative promises announcements, live demos across multiple devices, and focuses on Microsoft's vision of "unmetered intelligence"—running AI directly on PCs instead of relying exclusively on the cloud 00:01:01–00:01:12.

items.title: Build Apps w\/ Local AI for Unmetered Intelligence on every Windows PC
items.speakers: Jordi Janer, Aditi Narvekar, Anastasiya Tarnouskaya, Tucker Burns
items.sentence: Code walkthroughs reveal simplified model download, load, and inference routines for developers.

items.title: Build Apps w\/ Local AI for Unmetered Intelligence on every Windows PC
items.speakers: Jordi Janer, Aditi Narvekar, Anastasiya Tarnouskaya, Tucker Burns
items.sentence: This leads into a detailed explanation of Windows ML’s general availability and new tooling, notably the Windows ML CLI preview 00:21:28–00:27:00, which optimizes models through conversion, analysis, and benchmarking in one workflow.

items.title: Turn foundation models into production AI on Microsoft Foundry
items.speakers: Vivek Chauhan, Alicia Frame, Jetashree Ravi
items.sentence: Demo and Foundry Integration Announcement: The next section presents practical demos of Fireworks tools and the major announcement of its availability on Microsoft Foundry (00:14:42).

items.title: Orchestrate special agents with NVIDIA Nemotron models on Foundry
items.speakers: Joey Conway, AYSEN Ilkbahar, Stephen McCullough
items.sentence: Announced recently, Nemotron 3 Ultra integrates advanced training techniques like multi-teacher distillation (00:09:08) and new NVFP4 precision formats for NVIDIA Blackwell architecture, allowing one unified checkpoint for device compatibility.

items.title: Ship code faster with AI-powered NoSQL schema design
items.sentence: Marco and Sergei are introduced as the speakers who will discuss how to ship code faster using AI-powered NoSQL schema design.

items.title: Multi-agents in action with 3 AI agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
items.sentence: Introducing Azure Container App Sandboxes: The speakers discuss typical runtime problems—like cold starts, untrusted code execution, and lack of lifecycle control—and introduce Azure Container App Sandboxes as a solution (00:02:28–00:02:56).

items.title: Multi-agents in action with 3 AI agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
items.sentence: They announce a private preview of this feature, emphasizing its secure, isolated, and stateful infrastructure on demand.

items.title: Multi-agents in action with 3 AI agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
items.sentence: The presenters close with an invitation to upcoming sessions and demos at Build, thanking the audience for participating and reiterating Microsoft’s mission to make multi-agent orchestration secure, manageable, and developer-friendly.

items.title: Under the hood of Microsoft AI models
items.sentence: Conclusion and Availability: In closing at 00:16:48, Citron summarizes that Image 2.5, Voice 2, and Transcribe 1.5 are live today on Foundry and Microsoft’s Mai Playground, accessible even through mobile devices, while Thinking 1 is entering private preview through Foundry. "Code 1 Flash" is available in Visual Studio Code.

items.title: Turn APIs, tools, and data into real agent velocity
items.sentence: To address these challenges, the concept of “content understanding” is introduced as a method to transform messy, multimodal inputs into clean, structured outputs (00:01:47–00:02:13), formatted as Markdown or JSON containing agent-ready inputs.

items.title: Turn APIs, tools, and data into real agent velocity
items.sentence: The video closes by inviting viewers to explore full demo code on GitHub and attend a related breakout session on upcoming advancements like Generative Tech Mode and Foundry integration (00:21:19–00:21:47).

items.title: How Foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
items.sentence: Introducing Skills and Web Interaction: The conversation shifts to enhancing agent intelligence using “skills” — reusable playbooks defining sequences of actions like inbox triage (00:04:38).

items.title: How Foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
items.sentence: Demonstrations reveal trace replays showing user input, tool actions, and token costs across the workflow.

items.title: Build work-ready agents with Foundry + Work IQ, govern with Agent 365
items.sentence: He announces general availability targeting mid-June 00:03:11, promising full security compliance so agents only see appropriate data per identity permissions.

items.title: Any agent, any cloud: Standardized tracing with Foundry+OpenTelemetry
items.sentence: This establishes how Foundry’s observability reveals agent reasoning and behavior step by step.

items.title: From prompt to app, build AI powered apps on Windows
items.sentence: Optimizing AI Models with WinML: Around 00:09:38, the presenters unveil a newly released tool — WinML CLI.

items.title: Understand and fix Agent Framework apps with observability and evals
items.sentence: Through Phoenix, he shows how evals produce simple deterministic scores (supported/unsupported) and reveals that his agent is only correct around half the time.

items.title: Building a Multi-Agent Workflow in Microsoft Fabric
items.sentence: Agent SDK, Evaluation, and Governance: Alex moves deeper into technical territory by introducing the Data Agent SDK 00:19:45–00:21:01.

items.title: Data Science & Machine Learning with Microsoft Fabric
items.sentence: Introducing Fabric Data Agents: The next section shifts focus to Fabric’s data agent capabilities—a generative AI-powered feature that lets everyday users query their data intuitively without complex SQL writing (00:14:49).

items.title: Build automated agents using optimized AI Foundry models on Snapdragon
items.speakers: Darren Oberst, Meghana Rao, Morris Novello
items.sentence: Introduction and Motivation: At the start of the session 00:00:01–00:00:13, the presenters introduce the goal of demonstrating how to build automated AI agents using optimized models available in Microsoft Foundry for Snapdragon PCs.

items.title: Move AI workflows from test to production on Microsoft Foundry
items.sentence: The session ends with Manesh expressing gratitude for the presentation and sharing a brief announcement about the next upcoming session, encouraging participants to stay or have a great day 00:13:58–00:14:26.

items.title: Ship faster with Claude Code and Cowork in Microsoft Foundry
items.sentence: Caroline then announces the recent model release, Opus 4.8, describing it as Anthropic’s most advanced Frontier Intelligence offering ideal for accuracy-critical, long-horizon tasks 00:02:12.

items.title: Ship faster with Claude Code and Cowork in Microsoft Foundry
items.sentence: She introduces “dynamic workflows,” a new feature in Opus 4.8 enabling modular, sub-agent collaboration.

items.title: Ship faster with Claude Code and Cowork in Microsoft Foundry
items.sentence: First Frontier – Autonomy and Safety: Caroline discusses reducing human bottlenecks by introducing auto mode, a new classifier-based safety layer between model requests and execution permissions 00:06:35.

items.title: Create multimodal AI agents with persistent memory
items.sentence: Later, Microsoft’s representative joins to announce Napster’s public preview as a native Azure offering 00:04:38–00:05:05.

items.title: Orchestrate omnichannel AI agents with Foundry and Twilio
items.sentence: To solve scalability and security isolation issues, Microsoft introduced “hosted agents,” a new compute model built for persistent, secure, stateful sessions 00:05:22.

items.title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
items.sentence: Developers are introduced to the Surface RTX Spark Dev Box at 00:08:06, a machine delivering petaflop-level AI compute designed specifically for software creation.

items.title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
items.sentence: He unveils innovations like the AMD MI300 and the custom ARM-based Cobalt CPUs and networks designed for the dominant AI workloads: training, inference, and agent runtime.

items.title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
items.sentence: Stevie Bathiche and team reveal this as the platform for new "agent-first" computing—an ecosystem that connects specialized devices through Azure.

items.title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
items.sentence: The keynote concludes with the unveiling of the Majorana 2 quantum chip at 02:19:04, achieving breakthrough reliability and density for the coming quantum era.

items.title: Student to Startup
items.sentence: User Insights and Future Vision: In the final section, 00:09:09–00:13:13, Alicia reveals that her expected audience of young individual users evolved into mothers seeking solutions for their daughters’ hair.

items.title: What’s New in Azure Data: HorizonDB and Rayfin
items.speakers: Charles Feddersen, Sachin Patney, Nikisha Reyes-Grange
items.sentence: Charles, the Director of Program Management for Postgres and MySQL on Azure, and Sachin, the General Manager of App Development for Azure Data, join to share key data announcements made at Build.

items.title: What’s New in Azure Data: HorizonDB and Rayfin
items.speakers: Charles Feddersen, Sachin Patney, Nikisha Reyes-Grange
items.sentence: Azure Horizon DB Announcement: Charles explains that Azure Horizon DB is a newly announced Postgres-based database service designed for developers and enterprises 00:00:45.

items.title: What’s New in Azure Data: HorizonDB and Rayfin
items.speakers: Charles Feddersen, Sachin Patney, Nikisha Reyes-Grange
items.sentence: Microsoft is among the largest contributors to the upstream Postgres project, modifying roughly 8% of the codebase in the upcoming version 19 00:09:03.

items.title: Behind the Scenes: Accelerating the AI Agent DevOps Lifecycle with End-to-End Observability
items.speakers: Vivek Bhadauria, Nitya Narasimhan, Filisha Shah
items.sentence: The panel notes that “Agent Optimizer” is currently in gated preview and will soon be available in public preview 00:05:49–00:05:57, inviting developers to try it and provide feedback.

items.title: Toolboxes in Foundry: Build, Search and Govern Your Tools
items.speakers: Atul Aggarwal, Seth Juarez, Linda Li
items.sentence: Linda announces that Toolbox is currently in public preview and will achieve general availability later this month 00:13:17–00:13:34.

items.title: The Three IQs: Ground Your Agents in Knowledge, Data, and Work
items.sentence: Preventing Context Redundancy and Introducing Agent Templates: The next section discusses how IQs help reduce “context rot,” meaning developers no longer need to overload agents with excessive contextual data (00:05:00–00:05:17).

items.title: Intel quick take: Live from Microsoft Build
items.sentence: The host enthusiastically announces a special guest from NVIDIA, Steven, who will be sharing details about innovative projects his team has been developing.

items.sentence: This section transitions into discussions about Microsoft’s new “IQ” announcements—Work IQ, Foundry IQ, Fabric IQ, and Web IQ—which enable reasoning across multiple domains 00:02:10–00:02:21.

items.title: AI Building Blocks for .NET: Add intelligence to your C# apps
items.sentence: He introduces Aspire traces to monitor service interactions and sets the context for the upcoming deep dive into the .NET AI architecture.

items.title: AI Building Blocks for .NET: Add intelligence to your C# apps
items.sentence: Integrating External Tools with MCP: Bruno transitions to introducing the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard enabling AI applications to access external tools and APIs 00:23:01.

items.title: Powering the next AI frontier with a unified data platform
items.sentence: By 00:07:09, he announces new data connectors for SAP, Oracle, SharePoint, OneDrive, and previews for AWS Glue and Azure Monitor, reinforcing a “zero-ETL” approach for complete data unification.

items.title: Powering the next AI frontier with a unified data platform
items.sentence: Performance, Agent Skills, and AI Functions: Starting at 00:08:08, Amir unveils GPU-accelerated query performance in the Fabric Data Warehouse, achieving up to 8x to 100x improvements under concurrent workloads — a breakthrough showcased in multiple demos.

items.title: Powering the next AI frontier with a unified data platform
items.sentence: This naturally leads to the announcement of the general availability of “Planning in Fabric” at 00:22:00.

items.title: Powering the next AI frontier with a unified data platform
items.sentence: Finally, the narration transitions at 00:33:05 to “operations agents,” now generally available.

items.title: Take AI agents from prototype to production with OpenTelemetry
items.sentence: Built-in telemetry reveals “what” happened but not “why,” prompting the addition of custom spans and metrics for enriched context (00:07:55).

items.title: Develop a conversational search experience without rebuilding your app
items.sentence: Overview of Elastic Solutions: Jonathan Simon begins his portion at 00:02:04 by introducing himself and the structure of his presentation, which includes an overview, demo, and architectural walkthrough.

items.title: Develop a conversational search experience without rebuilding your app
items.sentence: He then officially announces the general availability of Elastic Agent Builder at 00:04:55, marking its transition from technical preview to production-ready status.

items.title: Your agent, anywhere: MultiClient, MultiDevice with GitHub Copilot SDK
items.sentence: Patrick outlines that they will discuss how to build and deploy with the SDK, touch on upcoming features, and share follow-up resources.

items.title: Your agent, anywhere: MultiClient, MultiDevice with GitHub Copilot SDK
items.sentence: He announces that the GitHub Copilot SDK has reached its General Availability milestone at version 1.0 (00:00:32–00:00:38), marking the end of a year-long journey from technical preview to official release.

items.title: Your agent, anywhere: MultiClient, MultiDevice with GitHub Copilot SDK
items.sentence: This shared “harness” ensures that experiences remain consistent across GitHub and Microsoft’s broader ecosystem, introducing a uniform Copilot feel across different products.

items.title: Inside Azure innovations with Mark Russinovich
items.sentence: Azure Boost is then introduced as a central innovation—it uses offload cards to move data paths for storage and networking from CPUs to dedicated DPUs, boosting throughput significantly.

items.title: Shipping custom models at scale from fine-tuning to inference
items.speakers: Rob Ferguson, Daniel Han, Mark Saroufin
items.sentence: Rob describes how elegant RL training recipes differ dramatically from actual infrastructure needs, emphasizing that math abstractions often reveal themselves as large GPU production costs.

items.title: Local models, developer control, and the future of AI runtimes
items.sentence: He notes partnerships with major model and hardware providers and announces that DeepMind’s new Jemma 4-12B unified model is already hosted and available on the O Llama platform (00:02:06–00:02:18).

items.title: Local models, developer control, and the future of AI runtimes
items.sentence: Upcoming hardware trends toward unified memory from NVIDIA, AMD, and Microsoft will further improve performance and model capacity (00:16:00–00:17:15).

items.title: Agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
items.sentence: He then transitions to upcoming AI research priorities (00:30:29): real-time responsiveness, memory and continual learning, and interactive micro-batching aimed at human-machine “mind melds.” This vision includes video generation driven by orchestrating agents and the rise of world models capable of predicting user preferences and intentions.

items.title: Building Agents You Can Trust on Windows
items.speakers: Kirupa Chinnathambi, Patrick Nikoletich, Stuart Schaefer
items.sentence: He reveals how users can toggle protection, customize network filters, and observe sandbox restrictions while maintaining developer flexibility across CLI, SDK, and integrated apps.

items.title: Building Agents You Can Trust on Windows

items.title: Stop routing docstrings to 70B models with on-device AI on Snapdragon
items.sentence: Introducing himself as the head of software strategy for Qualcomm’s compute business, he explains that his talk will be somewhat improvised, fitting for the creative “improv” venue (00:00:23).

items.title: WSL improvements and the new Containers CLI and APIs

items.title: WSL improvements and the new Containers CLI and APIs
items.sentence: CLI Demonstration and Basic Container Operations: Pooja demonstrates how WSL Containers can be accessed and managed using a new tool called WSLC, available after a simple WSL update (00:01:33).

items.title: WSL improvements and the new Containers CLI and APIs
items.sentence: Before closing, she mentions additional updates including Azure Linux 4.0’s availability and its upcoming inclusion as a WSL distribution (00:21:52).

items.title: Scale agentic AI cost‑efficiently on Azure with Arm Cobalt VMs
items.speakers: Gova Babu, Pranay Bakre, Sameer Nori
items.sentence: Goa announces that Cobalt 200 VMs, built on 3nm ARM architecture and optimized for Microsoft workloads, now ship with Azure Boost and achieve around 50% better per-core performance compared to the previous generation (00:03:35–00:04:12).

items.title: Scale agentic AI cost‑efficiently on Azure with Arm Cobalt VMs
items.speakers: Gova Babu, Pranay Bakre, Sameer Nori
items.sentence: The VMs are now available in preview across eight regions, with more expansion planned upon general availability (00:06:33–00:06:46).

items.title: Scale agentic AI cost‑efficiently on Azure with Arm Cobalt VMs

items.sentence: The segue to profiling is introduced by posing the challenge of measuring performance to optimize such multi-step, multimodal workflows 00:02:55–00:03:06.

items.title: Profile and optimize agentic AI on Windows
items.sentence: The results reveal trade-offs between speed and power consumption, allowing informed workload deployment strategies.

items.title: Develop faster on Windows with AI playbooks and local agents

items.title: Develop faster on Windows with AI playbooks and local agents
items.sentence: Conclusion and Call to Action: The session closes with an invitation to explore AMD’s Developer Portal (00:24:26–00:25:26), where visitors will find announcements on Halo, Lemonade updates, and Playbook workshops.

items.title: Secure agent workflows in GitHub Copilot with NVIDIA OpenShell
items.sentence: The speakers invite community testing and contributions, noting that the alpha version debuted at GTC in March and will advance to beta soon.

items.title: What’s real, ready, and next for developers with Scott Guthrie
items.sentence: Fabric IQ—now generally available—connects semantic data directly to Copilot in Microsoft 365 for more intelligent organizational insights (00:09:40–00:10:11).

items.title: What’s real, ready, and next for developers with Scott Guthrie
items.sentence: As AI agents and automated systems introduce millions of requests per second, the architecture must evolve beyond conventional relational designs to globally replicated systems capable of ultra-low latency.

items.title: What’s real, ready, and next for developers with Scott Guthrie
items.sentence: The video concludes as Seth thanks Guthrie, who confirms that the upcoming AI-driven transformation will be vast and exhilarating for developers and technologists alike (00:14:16–00:14:22).

items.sentence: The conversation concludes with Nate thanking Milos for joining Microsoft Build and encouraging viewers to explore AnyScale on Azure, which is now available in public preview (00:14:06).
